# CommonPub — Production-Readiness Audit

_Lead reviewer sign-off. 50 adversarially-confirmed findings. All file:line refs verified against source._

## 1. Verdict

**Not shippable as-is — there are four live P1 security defects that must clear before any production promotion.** The architecture is sound and the federation/RBAC scaffolding is mature, but the audit surfaced two independent stored-XSS vectors (unsanitized `javascript:`/`data:` URLs reaching `:href`), a horizontal-privilege leak on entrant PII, and — most serious — a **moderation-bypass where suspended/banned users simply re-authenticate with their own password**. Any one of these is a "do not deploy" gate; together they mean the auth and content-trust boundaries are not currently holding. Below the P1 line the codebase is in decent shape: the P2/P3 set is dominated by non-atomic multi-write sequences (count drift), a handful of DoS/unbounded-ingestion gaps, and a large but low-risk backlog of monolith decomposition, CSS/error-handling duplication, and stale docs. Recommendation: **fix the four P1 security items + the two highest-risk data-integrity P2s, then ship; schedule the monolith/DRY/docs work as fast-follow.**

## 2. P0 / P1 Blockers

_No P0s. Four P1 security blockers + two P1 monolith risks._

| Sev | Area | File:line | Issue | Fix |
|-----|------|-----------|-------|-----|
| P1 | Auth / moderation | `layers/base/server/api/auth/sign-in-username.post.ts:15` | **Ban/suspend is bypassable.** No login path checks `users.status`; suspended users have `deletedAt=null` so they re-authenticate and get a fresh session. `updateUserStatus('deleted')` doesn't even set `deletedAt`. | Enforce `status==='active'` in `middleware/auth.ts` `enrichUser` (null out ctx if not) **and** at login (Better Auth before-hook / reject in sign-in route). Make `updateUserStatus('deleted')` also set `deletedAt`. |
| P1 | Schema / XSS | `packages/schema/src/validators/_shared.ts:4` | `optionalUrl()` (and bare `z.string().url()` in `video.ts:9`, `hub.ts`) accepts `javascript:`/`data:` under zod 4.3.6 → stored XSS on rendered profile/hub website links (`u/[username]/index.vue:303`, `hubs/[slug]/index.vue:413/463`), under a CSP that allows `unsafe-inline`. | Add scheme allowlist refine (`protocol ∈ {http:,https:}`) to `optionalUrl`; apply to video/hub-resource URL. The team already learned this for `originPatternSchema` — apply it generally. |
| P1 | Federation / XSS | `layers/base/components/hub/HubResources.vue:121` | `:href="item.url"` with no scheme guard. Local creation only `z.string().url()`; federated sync (`hubMirroring.ts:1548/1558`) stores remote value verbatim with **zero** validation. Local path is live. | Scheme allowlist in `createHubResourceSchema` via `.refine()`; validate scheme in `syncFederatedHubCollection` before insert; safe-href helper on the binding. Same for product `purchaseUrl`/`datasheetUrl`. |
| P1 | Contest / authz | `layers/base/server/api/contests/[slug]/entries/[entryId]/private.get.ts:33` | Entrant-PII route authorizes on **instance-wide** `contest.pii`, not per-contest. A delegated contest-staff grant scoped to contest A can read contest B's entrant PII. Inconsistent with `files/[id]/raw.get.ts:48-62` and the registrants routes which require per-contest `canManage`. | Require `isEntrant \|\| (hasPermission('contest.pii') && (canManage \|\| isJudge))`, computing `canManage` exactly as the registrants-export route does. |
| P1 | Monolith (security-sensitive) | `packages/server/src/federation/inboxHandlers.ts:502` | `onCreate` is a ~500-line god-method dispatching 4 unrelated untrusted-AP flows; host/attribution checks are un-unit-testable in isolation. Highest-risk file in the audit. | Extract `handlePrivateNote` / `handleHubNote` / `handleFederatedContentCreate` / `handleReplyCreate`; `onCreate` → ~40-line dispatcher. |
| P1 | Monolith | `packages/server/src/federation/hubMirroring.ts:1` | 1634 lines / 28 exports tangling signed-HTTP transport, DB-repo CRUD, follow-protocol state machine, and engagement — pure DB tests drag in the fetch/signature surface. | Split into `hubMirrorRepo` / `hubFollowProtocol` / `hubPostIngestion` / `hubPostEngagement`, barrel-re-export to preserve API. |

## 3. Monolith & Duplication

**Decomposition (extract boundaries):**
- `federation/inboxHandlers.ts` `onCreate` → four private handlers (P1, above).
- `federation/hubMirroring.ts` → 4 modules by responsibility (P1, above).
- `server/src/content/content.ts` (1442 L) → move `forkFederatedContent`/`toggleFederatedBuildMark`/`isFederatedBuildMarked` into `content/federatedContent.ts`; `onContent*` hook emitters into `content/contentHooks.ts`. Leaves content.ts as local CRUD.
- `server/src/social/social.ts` (883 L) → `social/{likes,comments,bookmarks,follows,reports}.ts` behind the existing barrel (zero call-site churn). Isolating comment access-control aids testing.
- `components/contest/ContestEditor.vue` (405-L script) → `useContestMedia` + `useContestStatusMenu`; fold reseed watchers into `useContestEditor`. Target <150 L.
- `pages/admin/layouts/[id].vue` (569 L) → `useLayoutRowOps` + `useLayoutConflictResolution`.
- `components/LayoutSection.vue` → extract the ~100-line move-menu dropdown into a reusable `useMenuDropdown` (pattern is duplicated wherever a menu exists).

**DRY / duplication:**
- **CSS reader-chrome:** `ProjectView.vue:502-1535` (~1030 L scoped CSS) and `ArticleView.vue` (~680 L) duplicate 11 `.cpub-*` class families (`.cpub-prose`, byline, meta, engagement bar, cover). Lift shared chrome + prose into `packages/ui/theme` (`content-view.css`/`prose.css`) — the design source-of-truth per CLAUDE.md rule 3. Deletes several hundred drifting lines.
- **`.cpub-form-*` re-declaration:** 36 scoped rule-blocks across 11 files shadow the canonical `packages/ui/theme/forms.css` family (values already drift 10px/11px/`--text-sm`). Session-246 hoist is half-done; delete scoped copies, add missing `.cpub-form-field` to forms.css once. (`ContestEditor.vue:911` et al.)
- **Event pages:** `pages/events/create.vue` and `[slug]/edit.vue` are near-identical (shared template + byte-identical `<style>`). Extract `<EventForm>`; wrappers supply initial values + submit handler.
- **`errorMessage()` idiom:** `err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)` copy-pasted ~67× (server) — add one helper to `packages/server/src/utils.ts`. Client side: `useApiError().extract()` exists (15 callers) but 23 sites still hand-roll it — adopt it.
- **public/v1 list routes:** 14+ routes repeat the same `safeParse`+400 throw + pagination zod fields + `{items,total,limit,offset}` envelope. Add `paginationQuery` fragment + `parseListQuery`/`listEnvelope` helpers (or H3 `getValidatedQuery`).
- **Contest default-email copy:** duplicated in layer (`['paragraph',{html}]`) vs server (`['text',{text}]`) — already structurally diverged. Move raw copy strings to one dependency-free shared module; adapt block shape locally.
- **Inline authz:** `user.value?.role === 'admin'` hand-rolled in 3 pages — route through `useCan()`.

## 4. Cruft to Remove (safe deletions)

- **5 orphaned components (~345 L), zero references** (verified incl. dynamic/kebab/path-prefix names): delete `layers/base/components/{PostVoteButtons,TimelineItem,SkillBar,FormatToggle,ProgressTracker}.vue`. Only `PostVoteButtons` has a package import; confirm typecheck after. If vote UI is planned, keep it behind a tracked issue, not dead in the tree.
- **`buildContentUri`** (`federation.ts:799-805`, deprecated) — no non-test callers; remove it + 2 re-exports + its compat-test blocks (or add runtime `console.warn` + schedule next-minor removal if external API consumers exist).
- **`createAuthHook`** (`auth/src/hooks.ts:65`, deprecated alias) — zero consumers, not even a test. Delete it + the barrel re-export.

## 5. TS / Convention Debt (systemic)

- **`as any` at render boundaries** (`pages/u/[username]/[type]/[slug]/index.vue:97-99`, `ArticleView.vue:272/276`, `HubFeed.vue:128`, learn pages — ~13 casts / 8 files). This is the only real production `as any` cluster (the 121 total is otherwise tests). Each cast disables field-rename detection → silent blank-render/404, the exact bug class already in project memory. Fix: one shared `EnrichedContent`/`FeedItem` type in `@commonpub/schema`, passed typed into view props.
- **`@ts-ignore` instead of `@ts-expect-error`** for TS2589 useFetch suppressions (`useContentFeed.ts:68`, `useProfileContent.ts:34`, `ContentPicker.vue:23`, `CommentSection.vue:42`) — swallows any future error on those lines. Narrow by extracting a typed `useFetch` helper. (Naive swap breaks vitest per the vue-tsc-strict-vs-vitest memory.)
- **Hardcoded hex in `settings/appearance.vue:31-35`** — preset palettes as literals, duplicating `packages/ui/theme` tokens (violates CLAUDE.md rule 3). Source swatches from the theme package/`getComputedStyle`, or build-time-generate.
- **Schema strictness:** `configSchema` (`config/src/schema.ts:267`) and validators are not `.strict()` — typo'd config/flag keys silently stripped. DB feature-flag overrides (`apps/reference/server/utils/config.ts:125`) merged as `raw as Partial<FeatureFlags>` with no Zod re-validation. Free-text `$type` columns (`files.visibility`, `contestRegistrations.tier`, `registrationMode`) have no pg enum/CHECK — TS `$type` erases at runtime, so a bad write can leave a PII file non-`private` and slip its gate. Promote to `pgEnum`/CHECK and validate the override blob.

## 6. Docs Freshness

Current source values to write in (server **2.119.0** published, schema **0.61**, config **0.35**, layer **0.113** source / **0.112** published, test-utils **0.5.14**, infra **0.19**, migration **0045**, **38 flags**):

| Doc | Stale claim | Correct value |
|-----|-------------|---------------|
| `docs/reference/codebase-analysis.md:6-11` | server 2.117.3 / schema 0.60 / config 0.34 / layer 0.109 / test-utils 0.5.13; "migration 0043"; dated 2026-07-17 (s243) | server 2.119.0 / schema 0.61 / config 0.35 / layer 0.113 / test-utils 0.5.14; **migration 0045**; re-date to current session. Predates the entire rich-registration arc (s244 P1-P6) + s245. |
| `docs/STATUS.md:596-606` | "verified 2026-07-17 — s243" table: schema 0.60 / config 0.34 / server 2.117.3 / layer 0.109 | schema 0.61 / config 0.35 / server 2.119.0 / layer 0.112 — contradicts the file's **own** s245 TL;DR (lines 16-32). Refresh or replace with the `npm view` one-liner. |
| `docs/ROADMAP.md:19-24` | "Snapshot 2026-06-26"; email subsystem "entirely inert in prod" | False — s237 shipped email; **deveco.io runs Resend LIVE**. Re-snapshot against s229-245 (block-email editor, contest signup/reminders, rich registration all absent). |
| `CLAUDE.md` Apps table + `codebase-analysis.md:17-25` | Lists only `apps/reference` | `apps/shell` (@commonpub/shell, private v0.1.0) exists with its own build/dev/typecheck/db:migrate. Document it or delete the dir. |
| `docs/federation.md:1020` | codebase-analysis is "Always current" | Demonstrably stale (versions, mig 0043, open-vs-closed inbox). Soften to "periodically regenerated — verify against source." |

**Does `codebase-analysis.md` need a rewrite?** Yes — regenerate at minimum the baseline block, the migration number, the Apps section (add `apps/shell`), and the inbox-handler finding. It is labelled "canonical" but predates the two largest recent changes; a full regen against current source is warranted, not a spot-patch.

## 7. Prioritized Remediation Plan

**Batch 1 — SHIP GATE (P1 security; do before any prod promotion):**
1. Enforce `users.status` at login + in `enrichUser`; set `deletedAt` on delete (auth bypass).
2. Scheme-allowlist `optionalUrl` + video/hub URL validators (XSS root cause) — one shared http(s)-only helper.
3. Scheme guard on `HubResources.vue` `:href` + `syncFederatedHubCollection` insert + product URLs.
4. Per-contest scoping on the entry-private PII route.

**Batch 2 — data-integrity / abuse (high-value P2, ship soon after):**
5. Wrap non-atomic multi-writes in `db.transaction` + gate on rowcount: `deleteComment` (social.ts:476, recursive subtree), `shareContent` (`onConflictDoNothing`, posts.ts:759), `kickMember` (members.ts:549).
6. Fix comment/like counter mis-targeting for `lesson`/`video` (social.ts:378/126) — explicit per-type branch.
7. Cap ingestion: multipart Content-Length pre-check + nitro body cap (upload.post.ts:18); item/follower caps in `syncFederatedHubCollection` (hubMirroring.ts:1530).
8. Lock down `email-test.post.ts:92` (admin-only or own verified address, route through throttled outbox).
9. Remove `archived` from `learn` `PUBLIC_STATUSES` (index.get.ts:7).
10. `updateContestSchema` server-side date re-validation against merged row (contest.ts:337).

**Batch 3 — hardening (P3 security/schema):**
11. Add `accounts` UNIQUE(provider_id,account_id) + FK indexes; `sessions.user_id` index (migration).
12. Replace regex sanitizer with DOM-based (sanitize-html/DOMPurify) on federated-HTML ingest.
13. Digest/Signature multi-value header parsing (keypairs.ts:101) — federation interop.
14. `createReport` target/self/dup validation; pagination bounds on `listShares`/enrollments/certs/remote-members.
15. Nonce-based CSP; `.strict()` config + Zod-validate DB flag overrides; pgEnum/CHECK on security-relevant columns; product specs/pricing bounds.

**Batch 4 — structural (monolith + DRY, no runtime risk):** decompose the 6 monoliths (§3), lift shared CSS into `packages/ui/theme`, add `errorMessage()`/list-route helpers, delete the 5 orphan components + 2 deprecated aliases, extract `EnrichedContent` type + remove the `as any` cluster.

**Batch 5 — docs:** regenerate `codebase-analysis.md`; refresh STATUS table + ROADMAP snapshot; document/remove `apps/shell`; soften federation.md "Always current."

Batches 1-2 are the production path; 3-5 are fast-follow.
